Panji system?
They were categorised as 'Jaywar'.
3794
The Panji system identifies how many 'Moola Grams' (original 
villages) from which the 167 lineages spread?
One hundred and sixty-seven (167).
3795
How many main 'Moolas' were initially prioritised from the total 
167?
Thirty-four (34), which later became 35 with the inclusion of the 
Khandam branch.
3796
According to the source, why did Brahmins in India eventually 
divide into regional groups like 'Pancha Gauda' and 'Pancha 
Dravida'?
Due to geographical variations in climate, language, diet, and local 
cultural influences over time.
3797
Which five Brahmin groups constitute the 'Pancha Gauda' 
(Northern group)?
Utkala, Kanyakubja, Gauda, Maithil, and Saraswat.
3798
Which geographical feature serves as the boundary between 'Pancha 
Gauda' and 'Pancha Dravida' Brahmins?
The Vindhyachal mountain range.
3799
Followers of the Madhyandina branch of Yajurveda in Mithila are 
called _____.
Vajasaneyi
3800
Followers of the Kauthuma branch of Samaveda in Mithila are 
called _____.
Chandoga
3801
What is the major procedural difference between Chandoga and 
Vajasaneyi marriage rituals regarding timing?
Chandoga marriages have a specific 'Uttar Vivah' performed after 
sunset following the sighting of the Dhruva star, whereas 
Vajasaneyi rituals have no such timing restriction.
3802
The seventh-century work 'Tantra Varttika', which mentions 
'Samuha Lekhya' (family records), was written by _____.
Kumarila Bhatta
3803
Who was the scholar appointed by Harisimhadeva to organise the 
Panji for Maithil Brahmins?
Gunakar Jha
3804
Which two ancestors are shared between the Savarna and Vatsa 
gotras, preventing marriage between the two lineages?
Aurva and Chyavana (among others, indicating a common origin).
3805
The 'Chhatth' calculation involves searching for common ancestors 
across how many specific points of relationship?
Sixteen (16).
3806
In the Shrotriya category, how many 'Chaupals' or hierarchical 
grades are identified?
Eight (8).
3807
How many total 'Panjis' (books/records) are mentioned for the 
Shrotriya and Yogya/Panjibaddha groups?
185 (32 for Shrotriya and 153 for Yogya/Panjibaddha).
3808
What is 'Sakha Panji'?
A detailed record of the branches and sub-branches of a family 
lineage, tracking the expansion from the original Moola.
3809
The first 'Chhatth' point in marriage eligibility checks refers to 
which ancestor of the bride?
The father of the bride's great-great-grandfather (Vriddha 
Prapitamaha).
3810
Under what condition is a marriage prohibited even if the 
generation counts (5 or 6) are satisfied?
If the bride and groom share the same Gotra or the same Pravara.
3811
In the Panji system, what is the significance of the month from mid-
August to mid-September (Sun in Leo)?
It is the time prescribed for drying and preserving the Panji 
manuscripts using intense sunlight.
3812
The script traditionally used to maintain Panji records is _____.
Tirhuta (Mithilakshara)
3813
Which scholar is noted in the source for achieving first rank in the 
'Panji Shastra Martand' examination conducted by Maharaja 
Kameshwar Singh?
Modanand Jha
3814
How does the Panji system account for families that migrated to 
other regions like Bengal (e.g., Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's ancestors)?
Their origins are traced back to their original Mithila Moola (e.g., 
Khauwal Moola for Chaitanya).

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
3815
The term used for a girl from an unknown lineage or one whose 
mother's family is unrecorded is _____.
Agnyata (Unknown)
3816
Why is the marriage between Savarna Gotra and Vatsa Gotra 
forbidden?
They share common ancestors (Aurva, Chyavana, Bhargava, 
Jamadagnya, and Apnuvana) making them 'prohibited' relatives.
3817
According to the source, what is the role of the mother's brother's 
progeny in marriage rules?
A groom cannot marry the daughter of his maternal uncle (direct 
offspring of his mother's brother).
3818
The phrase 'Jalat Rakshet Tailat Rakshet' found in Panji 
manuscripts is an instruction to protect the book from what?
Water and Oil.
3819
Who was the scholar appointed to manage the Panji of the Karna 
Kayastha community?
Shankar Datta
3820
Who was the scholar appointed to manage the Panji of the 
Kshatriya community?
Vijay Datta
3821
The source compares the traditional Panji system to which modern 
scientific field?
Genome Mapping (due to its systematic tracking of hereditary traits 
and lineages).
3822
What is the 'Bijivara' or 'Bij' in the Panji system?
The inherent quality or status of a lineage, often subject to change 
based on marriage choices (Adhogami or descending status).
3823
What does 'Pachhwari Paat' refer to in the Maithil Brahmin social 
structure?
The broad group of Brahmins excluding the Shrotriya category.
3824
What is 'Pubari Paat'?
A term sometimes used to refer specifically to the Shrotriya group.
3825
In the ritual context of 'Pravara', what was the original purpose of 
invoking these ancestors during a sacrifice?
To invite Agni (the sacrificial fire) by reciting the names of 
distinguished sage-ancestors.
3826
How does the Panji system treat marriages that occurred with 
families from the 'West' (Paschim Desh)?
They are recorded as marriages into 'Agnyata Kula' (Unknown 
lineages) if the records do not align with the Panji.
3827
Term: Up-Tip
Definition: Supplementary genealogical notes used to update and 
track sub-branches of the Panji records.
3828
Which king's reign saw the introduction of 'Sakha Panji' to separate 
lineages based on social status changes?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
3829
What is the 'Adhogami' nature of the Panji status?
The concept that a family's rank within the Shrotriya/Yogya system 
can decrease if they marry into lower-ranked lineages.
3830
The 'Tantra Varttika' suggests that 'Samuha Lekhya' were 
maintained specifically to avoid forgetting ancestors of which two 
castes?
Kshatriyas and Brahmins.
3831
What was the significance of the village 'Mandu' for the Kashyapa 
Gotra?
It is the central Moola (ancestral root) for seventy-four different 
branches of that Gotra.
3832
What happens if a Panjikar discovers a 'Dushana' (fault) in a 
lineage during verification?
The marriage eligibility of the family might be compromised or 
their social ranking lowered.
3833
The term used for a female ancestor from a family of temple 
dancers or specific service groups is _____.
Devadasi
3834
How many ancestors are included in the 'Utegh' list to ensure no 
common ancestor is shared within prohibited degrees?
Thirty-two (32).
3835
Which ancestral line does the 16th 'Chhatth' point verify?
The maternal great-grandfather of the bride's maternal grandmother 
(Matri-matamahi-matamaha).
3836
The scholar Vidyapati Thakur belonged to which Moola?
Bisaphi (under the Kashyapa Gotra).
3837
According to the source, why did the Kshatriya Panji system 
eventually fall into disuse?
Primarily due to the passage of time and the loss of systematic 
institutional maintenance.
3838
Identify the primary source material used for writing Panji records 
before the widespread use of paper.
Tala-patra (Palm leaves).
3839
Which ancestral sages are common to the Shandilya gotra?
Shandilya, Asita, and Devala.
3840
Which ancestral sages are common to the Bharadwaja gotra?
Bharadwaja, Angirasa, and Barhaspatya.
3841
The scholar Mahesh Thakur, who founded the Darbhanga Raj, 
belonged to which Moola?
Kharaure (Saurath).
3842
In the context of Panji, what does the term 'Utegh' or 'Udegh' 
represent?
The introduction of 32 ancestral lineages required to verify a 
person's caste purity.
3843
According to the Panji system, how many generations must be 
avoided on the father's side for a valid marriage?
Seven generations.
3844
According to the Panji system, how many generations must be 
avoided on the mother's side for a valid marriage?
Five generations.
3845
Which historical King of Mithila is credited with formalising the 
Panji system in the $14$th century?
King Harisimhadeva.
3846
In what year (AD) was the decision made to formalise Panji records 
to prevent prohibited marriages?
$1326$ AD.
3847
Who was the first 'Panjikar' (registrar) appointed for the Maithil 
Brahmins?
Gunakar Jha.
3848
What specific incident involving Pandit Hari Nath Upadhyay led to 
the formalisation of Panji records?
He unknowingly married his cousin's daughter due to a lack of 
genealogical knowledge.
3849
Maithil Brahmins are divided into which two main Vedic branches 
based on their ritual practices?
Chhandoga (Samaveda) and Vajasaneyi (Yajurveda).

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
3850
The highest social tier in the Maithil Brahmin hierarchy established 
by the Panji is known as _____.
Shrotriya
3851
What are the four tiers of Maithil Brahmins defined by the Panji 
system?
Shrotriya, Yogya, Panjibaddh, and Jaywar.
3852
How many 'Mula Grams' (original villages) were identified during 
the initial Panji collection?
$167$.
3853
What defines a 'Biji Purush' in the Panji records?
The earliest known ancestor from whom a specific lineage or 'Mula' 
originates.
3854
In Panji terminology, what does 'Siddhanta Patra' (or 'Aswajan-
patra') signify?
A certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming that the bride and 
groom are not related within prohibited degrees.
3855
Lineages that are not of the same Gotra but share the same 
ancestors and cannot intermarry are called _____.
Sargotra
3856
Which Gotra includes the pravaras: Sandilya, Asita, and Devala?
Sandilya.
3857
The 'Vatsa' Gotra belongs to which pravara category based on the 
number of ancestors invoked?
Panch-Pravara (Five Pravaras).
3858
Which three Gotras are classified as 'Panch-Pravara' in the Maithil 
system?
Vatsa, Savarna, and Bhargava.
3859
How does the marriage of a 'Vajasaneyi' differ from a 'Chhandoga' 
regarding the timing of the 'Uttara Vivah'?
Chhandogas perform it after sunset after seeing the Arundhati star, 
whereas Vajasaneyis have no such time restriction.
3860
The rule stating a person cannot marry someone from their own 
Gotra is known as _____.
Sagotra Vivah Nishedh
3861
What are the six essential prohibited generational positions checked 
in the 'Chhadwa' rule?
The 6th position from 16 specific maternal and paternal ancestral 
lines.
3862
In the Panji system, the term 'Mula' refers to the _____.
Original ancestral village or lineage.
3863
Which Gotra consists of the lineages: Kashyapa, Avatsara, and 
Naidhruva?
Kashyapa.
3864
In the context of social status, 'Pachhwaripath' refers to Brahmins 
who are _____.
Not of the Shrotriya category (those who fall outside the highest 
tier).
3865
According to the Panji text, what happens to the children of a 
marriage that violates 'Svajana' (prohibited relation) rules?
They were historically considered 'Chandal' or socially boycotted 
from Aryan dignity.
3866
The term used for the written genealogical registers that track 
lineage quality and flaws is _____.
Samuh Lekhya
3867
Which philosopher mentioned 'Samuh Lekhya' in his work 'Tantra 
Vartika' to highlight the preservation of lineage?
Kumarila Bhatta.
3868
How did King Madhav Singh influence the Panji system in the 
$18$th century?
He initiated the 'Shakha Panji' and formalised the tiered 
classification of Shrotriya.
3869
In Karna Kayasthas, how many Gotras are traditionally recognised?
One (Kashyapa).
3870
What are the 'Pancha Gauda' Brahmin divisions mentioned in the 
text?
Utkala, Kanyakubja, Gauda, Maithil, and Saraswat.
3871
Which geographical feature divides the 'Pancha Gauda' from the 
'Pancha Dravida' Brahmins?
The Vindhyachal Mountains.
3872
Lineages belonging to the 'Ayan-Shrestha' (First Category) of the 35 
main mulas include _____.
Khadaure, Khauade, Budhabade, and Sodarpur.
3873
What is the 'Utegh Panji' specifically used for?
Identifying 'Sapinda' relationships by tracing six generations of 
ancestors.
3874
Concept: Pan Panji
Definition: A register that records the lineage and the specific 
original village (Mulagram) of ancestors.
3875
The first 'Chhadwa' position for a bride is her _____.
Paternal great-grandfather's paternal grandfather.
3876
The ninth 'Chhadwa' position for a bride is her _____.
Maternal grandfather's paternal great-grandfather.
3877
Why is marriage prohibited between the 'Savarna' and 'Vatsa' 
Gotras?
They share the same ancestors (Aurva, Chyavana, etc.), making 
them 'Sargotra'.
3878
The 'Dushan' or lineage flaw of 'Rajaka' refers to an ancestor 
marrying into a _____ family.
Washerman's
3879
The 'Dushan' of 'Charmakari' refers to an ancestor marrying into a 
_____ family.
Cobbler's
3880
Lineages like 'Khadaure', 'Madaie', and 'Darihare' belong to which 
ranking of Mulas?
Ayan Shrestha (First Tier).
3881
Lineages like 'Dighwe', 'Belanche', and 'Panchobhe' belong to which 
ranking of Mulas?
Madhyam (Middle Tier).
3882
Which Brahmin branch performs the 'Vedarambh' ceremony as one 
of their four primary sacraments?
Vajasaneyi (Yajurveda branch).
3883
The person from whom a lineage spreads into different villages 
while maintaining the same root is called the _____.
Biji Purush
3884
What rule did Mahamahopadhyaya Mahesh Thakur establish 
regarding paternal lineage marriage?
He deemed the 7th generation acceptable for marriage (as opposed 
to 8th).
3885
Which month of the year is traditionally recommended for drying 
Panji books to protect them from insects?
When the Sun is in Leo (approx. $16$ August to $15$ September).

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
3886
What is the meaning of 'Asapinda' in the context of marriage 
selection?
One who is not a 'Sapinda' (blood relative within restricted degrees) 
to the mother or father.
3887
The historical figure 'Lakhima' in the Panji records is associated 
with which Gotra and lineage?
Kashyapa Gotra, Sakaradi Mula.
3888
The term 'Laukika' in the Shrotriya community refers to _____.
The specific lineage rankings or categories within the Shrotriya 
Panji.
3889
How many 'Chhadwa' (prohibited ancestral positions) must be 
checked for both the bride and groom?
$16$ positions each (total of $32$).
3890
Which Gotra is associated with the Pravara 'Shakti, Vashistha, and 
Parashara'?
Parashara.
3891
Which Gotra is associated with the Pravara 'Angirasa, Vashistha, 
and Barhaspatya'?
Gautama.
3892
In the Panji, the 'Dushan' resulting from marriage into the family of 
a temple dancer is called _____.
Devdasi Dushan
3893
Which registrar was appointed for the Kshatriya community 
lineages?
Vijayadatta.
3894
Which registrar was appointed for the Karna Kayastha community 
lineages?
Shankardatta.
3895
The practice of 'Aruandhati Darshan' during marriage is specific to 
which group of Brahmins?
Chhandoga (Samaveda branch).
3896
The Panji system records historical titles; which lineage is 
associated with the title 'Mahamahopadhyaya'?
Various learned lineages including Sodarpur, Mandar, and Khauade.
3897
What does the term 'Jaywar' signify in the modern context of the 
Panji records?
Lineages whose detailed genealogical records are currently 
unavailable or incomplete.
3898
The rule for excluding relatives for marriage based on common 
ancestors is called the _____ rule.
Sapinda
3899
According to the Panji records, the lineage of the famous poet 
Vidyapati is traceable for at least _____ generations.
$7$ generations prior to him.
3900
The manual verification process where a Panjikar checks ancestors 
before a wedding is called _____.
Adhikar Janch (Authority Check)
3901
What is the 'Dushan' of 'Yavani'?
A lineage flaw resulting from marriage with a person of non-Hindu 
(Yavana/Muslim) background.
3902
Why is the Panji system compared to 'Genome Mapping' in the 
text's title?
Because it tracks hereditary traits, lineage, and genetic exclusivity 
across centuries.
3903
The 'Vishnuvriddha' Gotra features which specific Pravaras?
Vishnuvriddha, Kaunipuchha, and Trasadasyu.
3904
Which lineage is described as having $74$ branches originating 
from the Mandar village?
Kashyapa Gotra, Mandar Mula.
3905
The 'Pancha Dravida' Brahmin group includes which five regional 
communities?
Gurjara, Tailanga, Dravida, Maharashtra, and Karnata.
3906
Who provided the $16$th Chhadwa rule for the maternal 
grandmother's maternal grandmother's line?
The Panji tradition following the 'Chhadwa' algorithm.
3907
The Panji records for the Shrotriya community are divided into 
eight categories called _____.
Chaurals
3908
In Panji books, what does the mnemonic 'Jalat Raksha, Tailat 
Raksha' advise?
Protect the manuscript from water and oil.
3909
Which Gotra includes the Pravara 'Shandilya, Asita, and Devala'?
Shandilya.
3910
How did the Panji system respond to historical marriages into non-
Maithil or lower caste groups?
By recording them as 'Dushan' (flaws) to alert future generations 
during marriage negotiations.
3911
Who is credited with the formal initiation of the 'Panji Prabandh' 
genealogical system in Mithila in 1326 AD?
King Harisimhadeva of the Karnata dynasty.
3912
The Panji Prabandh was established to maintain the lineage purity 
of which three social groups?
Brahmins, Kayasthas, and Kshatriyas.
3913
According to the marriage laws of Panji Prabandh, a man is 
prohibited from marrying a woman within how many generations of 
his paternal lineage?
Seven generations.
3914
What is the status of Maithil Brahmins who are not formally 
recorded in the Panji registers?
Jaywar.
3915
Maithil Brahmins are divided into how many distinct 'Gotras' 
(clans)?
20
3916
In Maithil genealogy, what does the term 'Mula' designate?
The ancestral village or place of origin for a specific family lineage.
3917
How many original 'Mulas' (ancestral villages) were initially 
identified for Maithil Brahmins?
167
3918
What defines a 'Tri-Pravara' lineage in Maithil tradition?
A lineage whose three ancient ancestors were composers of 
Rigvedic hymns.
3919
Which specific Gotras are classified as 'Pancha-Pravara' (having 
five Vedic hymn-composing ancestors)?
Vatsa, Savarna, and Bharv.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
3920
Who was the first 'Panjikar' (genealogist) appointed by King 
Harisimhadeva for the Brahmin community?
Gunakar Jha.
3921
Which Panjikar was initially appointed to manage the genealogical 
records for the Karna Kayasthas?
Shankardatta.
3922
What is a 'Siddhanta Patra' (or Siddhanta)?
A formal certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming a couple's legal 
and genealogical right to marry.
3923
Maithil Brahmins primarily belong to which two Vedic schools?
Yajurveda (Madhyandina branch) and Samaveda (Kauthuma 
branch).
3924
Yajurvedic Maithil Brahmins of the Madhyandina branch are 
known by what title?
Vajasaneyi.
3925
Samavedic Maithil Brahmins of the Kauthuma branch are known 
by what title?
Chhandoga.
3926
The 'Vindhya' mountains historically serve as the boundary between 
the Pancha Gauda and the _____.
Pancha Dravida.
3927
Under which group of the 'Pancha Gauda' classification do Maithil 
Brahmins fall?
Maithil.
3928
In the context of the Panji system, what is the 'Biji Purush'?
The earliest known progenitor or founder of a specific lineage or 
'Mula'.
3929
What was the historical catalyst for King Harisimhadeva to 
formalise the Panji system in the 14th century?
A high-ranking scholar inadvertently marrying a close relative due 
to a loss of genealogical knowledge.
3930
Which philosopher's 7th-century work, the 'Tantra Varttika', first 
mentions 'Sammuha Lekhya' (collective lineage records)?
Kumarila Bhatta.
3931
The highest category in the Maithil Brahmin social hierarchy, 
strictly adhering to Panji rules, is called _____.
Shrotriya.
3932
The supplementary genealogical records used for tracking specific 
family branches are known as _____.
Shakha Panji.
3933
Which King ordered the creation of 'Shakha Panjis' (branch 
records) to further distinguish family rankings?
Maharaja Madhava Singh.
3934
In the calculation of marriage eligibility, how many specific 
relationship nodes, known as 'Chhatth', are tracked?
16
3935
If a shared ancestor is found within the paternal line of both the 
bride and groom at the 'Chhatth' level, the marriage is _____.
Forbidden.
3936
An overlap in the 'Chhatth' nodes is only genealogically permissible 
if it occurs within the _____.
Maternal lineage of the groom.
3937
The great-great-grandfather of the bride is historically identified as 
the _____.
First Chhatth (Prathama Chhatth).
3938
Which script was traditionally used to write and preserve the 
Maithil Panji records?
Tirhuta (also known as Mithilakshar).
3939
Maithil Brahmins following the Samavedic tradition perform the 
'Uttara Vivaha' (second part of the wedding) only after observing 
which star?
Arundhati.
3940
The 'Laukika' refers specifically to the lineage records and status of 
which group?
Shrotriya Brahmins.
3941
Which Gotra is associated with the 'Mandane' Mula, spanning 74 
branches in Mithila?
Kashyap.
3942
What term describes the downward shift in family status resulting 
from marriage into a lower-ranked lineage?
Adho-gami.
3943
According to the 'Dushana Panji', why were certain lineages given 
the title 'Taskar' (Thief)?
The genealogists added the title after a man abducted and married a 
woman to protect the Panji's integrity.
3944
How does the Panji system view marriage with the daughter of a 
maternal uncle?
It is strictly forbidden as a violation of genealogical purity.
3945
In the Panji system, what is the 'Pachhwari-paat'?
A collective term for the Brahmin classes ranked below the 
Shrotriya and Yogya divisions.
3946
What is the literal meaning of 'Panji Prabandh'?
The management or arrangement of genealogical registers.
3947
The ancestor who lived circa 400 AD and is considered the Biji 
Purush of the Mandan Mula is _____.
Trivenir Bhatta.
3948
Which Mula group, including Kharaure and Khauware, occupies 
the 'Ayan-Shreshtha' (Foremost Excellent) rank?
The first category of the 35 primary Mulas.
3949
What is the 'Mula-Gram'?
The specific village identified as the primary residence of the 
progenitor of a clan.
3950
How are Panji books traditionally preserved to prevent damage 
from moisture and pests?
They are dried in the sun when it enters the sign of Leo (Sinha 
Rashi).
3951
The term 'Sapinda' refers to relatives sharing a common _____.
Ancestral offering or body (Pinda).
3952
Which class of Brahmins is identified as 'Yogya'?
The second highest rank in the Maithil social order, below Shrotriya 
but above Panjibaddh.
3953
What scriptural rule is the foundation for the 5 and 7 generation 
marriage restrictions in Mithila?
Matritah Panchami, Pitritah Saptami (from the Manu Smriti).

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
3954
In the Panji system, what is the significance of 'Brahmapura'?
It is a rare Mula found across multiple Gotras, such as Shandilya, 
Vatsa, and Kashyap.
3955
What does 'Genome Mapping' signify in the title of the provided 
text?
A modern metaphorical comparison to the ancient, precise 
genealogical tracking of the Panji system.
3956
Why is marriage prohibited for someone designated as 'Svajana'?
They are considered too closely related, and offspring of such 
unions were historically likened to 'Chandalas'.
3957
Which ruler is credited with institutionalising the Panji system in 
Mithila?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva of the Karnat dynasty.
3958
In what year (Shaka and AD) was the Panji Prabandh formally 
established?
Shaka 1248, which corresponds to 1326 AD.
3959
According to the Panji system, how many generations must be 
avoided for marriage on the paternal side?
Six or seven generations.
3960
The earliest reference to written lineage records ('Samuha Lekhya') 
is found in which 7th-century text?
The 'Tantra Vartika' by Kumarila Bhatta.
3961
What term refers to the earliest known ancestor of a specific lineage 
in the Panji system?
Beeji Purush.
3962
What does the term 'Mulgram' signify in Maithil genealogy?
The original or ancestral village of a family's lineage.
3963
Into which two major Vedic branches are Maithil Brahmins 
primarily divided?
Samavedi (Chhandogya) and Shukla Yajurvedi (Vajasaneyi).
3964
What is the difference in marriage timing between the Chhandogya 
and Vajasaneyi branches?
Chhandogya marriages occur in two parts (East and West), while 
Vajasaneyi marriages have no such division.
3965
Define 'Tri-pravara' in the context of Maithil Gotras.
A Gotra whose ancestors composed three specific hymns or 
sections of the Rigveda.
3966
Define 'Panch-pravara' in the context of Maithil Gotras.
A Gotra whose ancestors composed five specific hymns or sections 
of the Rigveda.
3967
Which three Gotras are classified as Panch-pravara?
Vatsa, Savarna, and Garga.
3968
Who were the three sages associated with the Shandilya Gotra?
Shandilya, Asit, and Deval.
3969
List the three sages (Pravaras) associated with the Kashyap Gotra.
Kashyap, Avatsara, and Naidhruva.
3970
Who was the scholar appointed by Harisimhadeva to manage 
Brahmin Panji records?
Gunakar Jha of the Pandua Mul.
3971
Who was appointed to manage the Panji records for the Karna 
Kayasthas?
Shankardatt.
3972
Who was the first registrar appointed for the Kshatriya Panji 
records?
Vijaydatt.
3973
What are the four categories of Maithil Brahmins created by the 
Panji system?
Shrotriya, Yogya, Panjibaddh, and Jaywar.
3974
What is the 'Siddhanta Patra' (or 'Asvajan Patra')?
A certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming that a couple is not 
related within prohibited degrees.
3975
What information is contained in a family's 'Utivedh'?
A complete ancestral introduction covering 32 distinct maternal and 
paternal lineage points.
3976
How many 'Muls' (root lineages) were originally identified for 
Maithil Brahmins?
167
3977
Out of the 167 original Muls, how many were designated as 
'Mukhya' (main) Muls?
34 (later expanded to 35).
3978
The 'Panch Gaur' Brahmin group includes Maithils, Saraswats, 
Kanyakubjas, Gaurs, and _____.
Utkals.
3979
Which geographical feature divides the 'Panch Gaur' from the 
'Panch Dravid' Brahmins?
The Vindhyachal mountain range.
3980
What is the status of a child born from a marriage between 
prohibited relatives (Svajana)?
The child was historically considered 'Chandala' and excluded from 
Aryan social rites.
3981
Term: Chhad-vith
Definition: The 16 specific ancestral lines checked to ensure 
marriage eligibility.
3982
Who is the primary Chhad-vith checked for a bride's eligibility?
The father of the bride's great-great-grandfather.
3983
Which Gotra is the only one found among Karna Kayasthas?
Kashyap.
3984
Which type of Panji record tracks family defects or social lapses?
Dushan Panji.
3985
What is the function of the 'Shakha Panji'?
It provides a detailed branch-wise genealogy including titles, 
education, and migrations.
3986
Who introduced the 'Shakha Panji' during his reign in the 18th 
century?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
3987
What script is traditionally used to write Panji records?
Tirhuta (Mithilakshar).
3988
Which Brahmin category is also known as 'Pubaripar'?
Shrotriya.
3989
Which Brahmin category is also known as 'Pachhbaripar'?
The remaining categories excluding Shrotriyas (Yogya, Panjibaddh, 
Jaywar).
3990
How many 'Chouvala' (sub-categories) exist within the Shrotriya 
Panji?
Eight.
3991
What happens to the rank of a Shrotriya lineage if they marry into a 
lower category?
The lineage rank descends (Adhogami) and may eventually lose 
Shrotriya status.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
3992
What is the 'Asvajan Patra' specifically intended to prove?
That the bride and groom are not 'Svajana' (relatives within 
prohibited degrees).
3993
How does a 'Panjikar' traditionally preserve Panji books from 
damage?
By protecting them from water, oil, and loose binding, and drying 
them in the sun during the month of Bhadra.
3994
In the context of 'Chhad-vith', the 10th check refers to whom?
The maternal grandfather of the bride's paternal great-grandmother.
3995
Explain the significance of the 7th Chhad-vith.
It tracks the paternal great-grandfather of the bride's paternal 
grandmother.
3996
Why did the Panji system decline among the Kshatriya (Rajput) 
community in Mithila?
Due to the loss of written records and shifts in social practices over 
time.
3997
What major legal/religious texts define the marriage rules used in 
the Panji system?
The Manusmriti and Yajnavalkya Smriti.
3998
How many sages are mentioned in the Pravara for the Parashar 
Gotra?
Three (Shakti, Vashistha, and Parashar).
3999
Which Gotra is associated with the sages Angira, Vashistha, and 
Barhaspatya?
Gautam.
4000
The 'Laukik' system is primarily associated with which Brahmin 
rank?
Shrotriya.
4001
What was the historical consequence of a 'Panjikar' issuing a false 
eligibility certificate?
They were given titles like 'Taskar' (thief) as a mark of disgrace.
4002
Which Panji type specifically details the 'Sapinda' relationship to six 
generations?
Utivedh Panji.
4003
What is the 'Ayanthi Shrestha' category in the hierarchy of Muls?
The first or highest grade of ancestral roots, including Muls like 
Kharaure and Khauare.
4004
What determines if a 'Chhad-vith' relationship is permissible for 
marriage?
A Chhad-vith ancestor is prohibited if found in the groom's paternal 
line, but permissible if found in the groom's maternal line.
4005
Which Gotra is associated with the sages Katyayan, Vishnu, and 
Angira?
Katyayan.
4006
Which Gotra includes the sages Vishnuvriddha, Paurukutsa, and 
Trasadasyu?
Vishnuvriddha.
4007
Name the sages in the Bharadwaj Gotra's Pravara.
Bharadwaj, Angira, and Barhaspatya.
4008
What is the 16th Chhad-vith check?
The maternal grandfather of the bride's maternal grandmother's 
maternal grandmother.
4009
In the context of Mithila, what is the 'Panji Prabandh'?
A comprehensive system of genealogical records used to maintain 
caste purity and manage genetic mapping.
4010
Which historical ruler is credited with the formal initiation of the 
Panji Prabandh in Mithila?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
4011
According to the source material, in which year (Shaka and AD) 
was the Panji Prabandh decision formalised?
Shaka 1248, which corresponds to 1326 AD.
4012
Who was appointed to manage the Panji records for the Maithil 
Brahmins?
Gunakar Jha.
4013
Who was the designated Panjikar for the Karna Kayasthas during 
Harisimhadeva's reign?
Shankardutt.
4014
Who was the primary record keeper appointed for the Kshatriyas in 
the Panji system?
Vijaydutt.
4015
What is the primary purpose of 'Genome Mapping' (Genome 
Mapping) mentioned in the Maithil Panji context?
To ensure the purity of lineage and prevent marriage within 
prohibited degrees of kinship.
4016
The Panji system originally covered three social groups: Brahmins, 
Kayasthas, and _____.
Kshatriyas.
4017
What is the term for the 32-generation lineage introduction required 
for marriage eligibility in the Panji system?
Utegh.
4018
How many total 'Gotras' are recognised among Maithil Brahmins 
according to the text?
20
4019
How many 'Gotras' are considered well-established and numerically 
dominant in Maithil society?
7
4020
What is a 'Moola' in the Panji system?
The original village or root settlement of a specific lineage.
4021
How many 'Moola-grams' (original villages) are recorded in the 
Maithil Brahmin Panji?
167
4022
What is the 'Pravara' of a Gotra?
The list of distinguished sage ancestors who composed Vedic 
hymns, usually numbering three or five.
4023
How does a 'Tri-pravara' differ from a 'Panch-pravara'?
A Tri-pravara has three distinguished sage ancestors, while a Panch-
pravara has five.
4024
The Gotras Vatsa, Savarna, and Bharadwaj are classified as having 
_____ pravaras.
Five.
4025
In the Panji system, what is 'Sapinda'?
The state of sharing the same body particles/bloodline within a 
specified number of generations.
4026
For marriage eligibility, how many generations must typically be 
avoided on the father's side?
Seven generations (or six in some revised interpretations).

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
4027
For marriage eligibility, how many generations must be avoided on 
the mother's side?
Five generations.
4028
What is a 'Siddhanta' (or Ashvajana-patra) in the Panji tradition?
A formal certificate of eligibility issued by a Panjikar confirming 
that the bride and groom are not prohibited relatives.
4029
Which official is authorised by the state to verify genealogical 
records and issue marriage certificates?
The Panjikar.
4030
Maithil Brahmins are followers of which two Vedas?
Yajurveda and Samaveda.
4031
Yajurvedic Maithil Brahmins following the Madhyandina branch 
are known as _____.
Vajasaneyi.
4032
Samavedic Maithil Brahmins following the Kauthuma branch are 
known as _____.
Chhandogya.
4033
Which legendary Maithil poetâ€™s lineage is extensively tracked in 
the Panji records?
Vidyapati Thakur.
4034
The Brahmin category 'Shrotriya' was distinguished from others 
during the reign of which later Maharaja?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
4035
What are the three main social categories of Maithil Brahmins 
derived from the Panji system?
Shrotriya, Yogya, and Panjibaddha (or Jaibar).
4036
Which script is traditionally used to write the Panji records in 
Mithila?
Tirhuta (or Mithilakshar).
4037
What does the term 'Chhadwith' refer to in Panji terminology?
The sixteen specific prohibited relationship points/ancestors that 
must be checked for marriage eligibility.
4038
Why were the Panji records traditionally dried in the sun during the 
month when the Sun enters Leo?
To protect the palm leaves (Talpatra) from moisture and pests using 
the intense heat.
4039
What was the consequence of a marriage between 'Svajanas' 
(prohibited relatives) according to historical social norms?
The offspring were considered 'Chandala' and the family was 
ostracised from the Aryan social order.
4040
Who wrote the 'Varna-Ratnakara', which mentions the lineages and 
the reign of Harisimhadeva?
Jyotirishwar Thakur.
4041
In the Karna Kayastha community, how many Gotras exist 
according to the text?
Only one (Kashyap).
4042
How does the Panji record distinguish between 'Moola' and 'Gram'?
Moola is the ancestral root village, while Gram is the current place 
of residence.
4043
The Gotra 'Vatsa' and the Gotra 'Savarna' share the same ancestors; 
how does this affect marriage?
Members of these two Gotras cannot intermarry as they are 
considered kin.
4044
What historical event led to the formalisation of the Panji records in 
the 14th century?
The marriage of Pandit Harinath Upadhyaya to his maternal cousin 
due to lack of recorded genealogical knowledge.
4045
What is the significance of the year 450 AD mentioned in the 
document?
It marks the earliest historical reference point tracked in some 
ancient Panji lineage management theories.
4046
How did regional culture influence Brahmin divisions intoUtkala, 
Kanyakubja, Gauda, Maithil, and Saraswat?
Differences in climate, language, diet, and attire led to the 
categorisation of the 'Panch Gauda'.
4047
According to Kumarila Bhatta, why were 'Samuha-Lekhyas' (group 
records) maintained?
To ensure that the noble traditions and the purity of the family were 
not forgotten.
4048
Which Brahmin category is also referred to as 'Pachhwari-pa' in the 
later hierarchical system?
Panjibaddha (those below the Shrotriya and Yogya ranks).
4049
What is the 'Laukika' in the context of Shrotriya Panjis?
The specific social rank or status assigned based on marriage 
alliances and lineage purity.
4050
The 'Siddhanta' ceremony ensures that a bride is _____ (non-
Sapinda).
Asapinda.
4051
What specific rule applies to the marriage of a man with the 
daughter of his mother's brother?
It is strictly prohibited in the Panji system.
4052
In which book did Kumarila Bhatta first mention the use of written 
family records in the 7th century?
Tantra Vartika.
4053
What is the role of 'Mithilakshar' in the preservation of the Panji?
It is the original script used for all traditional genealogical 
manuscripts, serving as their linguistic soul.
4054
How did the Panji system adapt to migration?
It tracked the 'Moola' (origin) while recording the new 'Gram' 
(residence) as a suffix (e.g., Mandar-Sarhad).
4055
The 20 Gotras of Maithil Brahmins are divided based on their 
number of _____.
Pravaras.
4056
What is the 'Utegh' record of the maternal side specifically called?
Matripaksha Utegh.
4057
Which Maharaja of Darbhanga commissioned the 'Shakha Panjis' 
as they are known today?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
4058
True or False: The Panji system is currently still active among the 
Maithil Brahmins and Karna Kayasthas.
True.
4059
What is the 'Asvajanapatra' document?
Another name for the Siddhanta or the document certifying that the 
parties are not related.
4060
In what year was the Panji Prabandha (genealogical management 
system) formally established in Mithila?
1326 AD (Shaka 1248).
4061
Which ruler of the Karnat dynasty is credited with the initiation of 
the Panji Prabandha?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
4062
The Panji records were historically maintained for which three 
social groups in Mithila?
Brahmins, Kayasthas and Kshatriyas.
4063
What is the primary purpose of the Mithila Panji system regarding 
marriage?
To ensure 'Jati Shuddhi' (caste purity) and avoid prohibited degrees 
of relationship.
4064
How many 'Gotras' are recognised within the Maithil Brahmin 
community according to the Panji?
20
4065
According to the Panji system, how many maternal generations 
must be excluded to determine marriage eligibility?
Five.
4066
According to the Panji system, how many paternal generations must 
be excluded to determine marriage eligibility?
Seven (often reduced to six for certain categories).
4067
Term: Siddhant Patra
Definition: A formal certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming that 
a couple has no prohibited genealogical links for marriage.
4068
Which scholar was initially appointed by Harisimhadeva to manage 
the Panji for Maithil Brahmins?
Pandit Gunakar Jha.
4069
In the Panji system, what does the term 'Uderh' represent?
The detailed introduction of 32 ancestral lines (maternal and 
paternal) used to verify marriage eligibility.
4070
The 'Panch Gauda' group of Brahmins includes Utkal, Kanyakubja, 
Gauda, Saraswat and _____.
Maithil.
4071
Geographically, the 'Panch Gauda' Brahmins reside to the _____ of 
the Vindhyachal mountains.
North.
4072
How many 'Mools' (ancestral roots) were initially recorded in the 
Maithil Brahmin Panji?
167
4073
Followers of the Madhyandina branch of the Yajurveda in Mithila 
are known as _____.
Vajasaneyi.
4074
In Samavedi (Chhandogya) marriages, which phase occurs after 
sunset following the sighting of the star Arundhati?
Uttara Vivaha.
4075
According to the Smritis of Manu and Yajnavalkya, marriage is 
forbidden with a girl who is a _____ (sapinda) of the mother or 
father.
Blood relative.
4076
What incident allegedly triggered the formalisation of the Panji 
system in the 14th century?
Pandit Harinath Upadhyaya unknowingly married his first cousin 
(paternal aunt's daughter).
4077
How many Pravaras are associated with the 'Sandilya' Gotra?
Three (Tri-pravara: Sandilya, Asit, Deval).
4078
Marriage is prohibited between the Vatsa and _____ Gotras because 
they share common ancestors.
Savarna.
4079
The 16 'Chhatthis' in the Panji refer to 16 specific _____ whose 
lineages are checked for commonality.
Ancestral points (great-grandparents and beyond).
4080
In the 'Uderh' process, what is the 'First Chhatthi'?
The father's paternal grandfather's father (Great-great-grandfather).
4081
What are the three main hierarchical categories of Maithil Brahmins 
mentioned in the Panji context?
Shrotriya, Yogya and Panjibaddha.
4082
Under Maharaja Madhav Singh, a more complex hierarchical 
division was introduced called the _____ Panji.
Shakha (Branch).
4083
Which ancient philosopher's 'Tantravarttika' mentions 'Samuha 
Lekhya' (community records) as a means of preserving lineage 
purity?
Kumarila Bhatta.
4084
In Mithila, the village where the 'Beeji Purush' originally resided is 
called the _____.
Moolgram.
4085
According to the text, what is the 'Panch-pravara' (five sages) list 
for the Vatsa Gotra?
Aurva, Chyavana, Bhargava, Jamadagnya and Apnuvana.
4086
Why is the Panji system often compared to modern 'Genome 
Mapping' in recent academic discourse?
Due to its systematic tracking of hereditary biological information 
over centuries.
4087
To protect the Panji manuscripts (Pothis) from decay, they were 
traditionally kept away from water, oil and _____.
Loose binding.
4088
What is the prohibited degree of kinship known as in the Panji 
terminology?
Swajanita.
4089
The 'Tri-pravara' Gotras are those whose ancestors composed 
hymns in the _____.
Rigveda.
4090
Within the 167 original Mools, how many were considered 
'Mukshya' (primary) Mools?
34 (later 35 with the addition of the Kharaur branch).
4091
What is the specific prohibition regarding the 'Nimata' (mother's 
brother's children) in Mithila marriage rules?
Marriage with the offspring of the mother's brother is strictly 
prohibited.
4092
The Panjikar verifies that the bride and groom are not related within 
_____ generations on the father's side.
Seven.
4093
How many 'Chauvala' (classes) are the Shrotriya Panjis divided into? Eight.
4094
The term 'Pachhwaripaat' refers to Brahmins belonging to which 
group?
Those outside the Shrotriya category (Yogya and Panjibaddha).
4095
Term: Mool Panji
Definition: The original record listing all established ancestral roots 
and their founding members.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
4096
The Panji system records historical instances of marriages with 
'Devadasis' or individuals from 'Unknown castes' under which 
specific Panji type?
Dushana Panji (records of flaws/exceptions).
4097
Which historical figure's lineage is traced in the Panji as the 'Beeji' 
for the Shishiaram Mool?
Mahamahopadhyaya Halayudha.
4098
According to the Panji calculations, what is the 'Fifth Chhatthi'?
The paternal grandmother's great-grandfather.
4099
The term 'Beeji' in a Panji entry refers to the count of _____ from 
the founding ancestor to the current individual.
Generations.
4100
What is the role of the star 'Arundhati' in the Samavedi marriage 
ritual?
It must be sighted after sunset during the 'Uttara Vivaha' phase.
4101
The Madhyam Mool group includes lineages such as Digahve, 
Belonche and _____.
Ekhatar (Ekhatte).
4102
In the Karna Kayastha Panji, how many Gotras are primarily 
recognised?
One (Kashyapa).
4103
A marriage that violates Panji rules is considered _____ 
(unscriptural) and leads to social exclusion.
Ashastriya.
4104
The term 'Asvajan Patra' is a synonym for which marriage 
document?
Siddhant Patra.
4105
The Panji system documents the movement of Maithil Brahmins to 
other regions like Bengal; for example, the ancestors of _____ 
Mahaprabhu.
Chaitanya.
4106
Which Maharaja of Darbhanga requested the creation of 'Shakha 
Panji' to differentiate Shrotriyas from others?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
4107
The 16th Chhatthi in the genealogical verification is the father's 
maternal grandmother's _____.
Maternal grandfather.
4108
What does the Panji record about the descendants of a 'Swa-janita' 
marriage?
They are considered spiritually and socially degraded ('Chandala' 
status).
4109
Which Panjikar was awarded the title 'Panji-Shastra Martand' by 
the Maithili Academy?
Modanand Jha.
4110
The concept of 'Sapinda' refers to relatives sharing the same _____.
Body/Ancestral particles (as defined in Hindu law).
4111
According to the Panji, the Vatsa and Savarna Gotras are forbidden 
to intermarry due to common _____.
Pravaras.
4112
How many Pravaras are associated with the 'Kashyapa' Gotra?
Three (Kashyapa, Avatsara, Naidhruva).
4113
What is the 'Seventh Chhatthi' in the marriage eligibility checklist?
The paternal grandmother's maternal grandfather.
4114
According to the text, the Panji system preserves records spanning 
over _____ years of history.
1000
4115
What is the primary objective of the 'Mithila Panji Prabandh' 
system established in the medieval period?
To ensure the genealogical purity (Jati Shuddha) of Brahmins, 
Kayasthas, and Kshatriyas.
4116
Under which king's patronage was the Maithil Panji system 
formally organised in 1326 AD?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
4117
According to the marriage rules in the Panji records, how many 
generations must be avoided on the father's side (Pitripaksha)?
Seven generations.
4118
According to the marriage rules in the Panji records, how many 
generations must be avoided on the mother's side (Matripaksha)?
Five generations.
4119
The Panji records identify three main hierarchical categories for 
Brahmins: Shrotriya, Yogya, and _____.
Panjibaddh
4120
What term is used to describe those who are not included in the 
official Panji genealogical records?
Jaibar (or Jaybar).
4121
How many total 'Gotras' are identified among the Maithil Brahmins 
in the source material?
Twenty (20).
4122
What is the specific meaning of a 'Tri-Pravara' lineage?
A lineage whose ancestors are credited with composing three 
Rigvedic hymns (Suktas).
4123
What is the specific meaning of a 'Panch-Pravara' lineage?
A lineage whose ancestors are credited with composing five 
Rigvedic hymns (Suktas).
4124
Name the five northern Brahmin groups (Pancha Gauda) mentioned 
in the Puranas.
Utkala, Kanyakubja, Gauda, Maithila, and Saraswata.
4125
In genealogical terms, what is a 'Beeji Purusha'?
The original progenitor or earliest known ancestor from whom a 
lineage descends.
4126
What does 'Moolagrama' signify in the Panji records?
The original village or place of ancestral residence of a family.
4127
Who was appointed to manage the genealogical records for the 
Karna Kayasthas?
Shankaradatta.
4128
Who was appointed to manage the genealogical records for the 
Kshatriyas in the Panji system?
Vijayadatta.
4129
The certificate issued by a Panjikar to confirm a marriage is valid 
and 'non-related' is called _____.
Siddhant Patra (or Ashvajana-patra).
4130
How many 'Moolas' (original root families) were primarily 
identified among Maithil Brahmins?
One hundred and sixty-seven (167).

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
4131
The professional genealogists who maintain and verify the Panji 
records are known as _____.
Panjikars
4132
What historical event in the 14th century prompted the 
formalisation of the Panji records?
The marriage of Pandit Harinath Upadhyaya into a prohibited 
degree of relationship due to ignorance of lineage.
4133
In which script were the traditional Panji records primarily written?
Tirhuta (Mithilakshar).
4134
Why is marriage prohibited with the child of a maternal uncle 
(Mama) in the Maithil system?
It is a violation of the 'Sapinda' marriage rules specified in the 
Smritis.
4135
What defines the 'Shakha Panji' specifically?
A detailed record tracing a lineage from the Beeji Purusha down to 
current descendants, including titles and education.
4136
Under the rules of Maharaja Mahesh Thakur, the paternal exclusion 
for marriage was adjusted from seven generations to _____ 
generations.
Six generations (making the seventh generation eligible).
4137
What specific ecological rule was suggested for the preservation of 
Panji manuscripts?
They should be dried in the sun when the Sun is in the Leo (Simha) 
constellation (approx. August-September).
4138
The 'Laukika' refers specifically to the marriage records of which 
Brahmin category?
Shrotriya.
4139
How many 'Chauris' (sub-categories) exist within the Shrotriya 
Panji?
Eight (8).
4140
Which Maithil scholar of the 7th century is cited as an early 
reference for 'Samuha Lekhya' (group records)?
Kumarila Bhatta.
4141
What historical period saw the decline of 'Samuha Lekhya' before 
the 1326 AD re-organisation?
The late 13th century (prior to Harisimhadeva's reign).
4142
Which Veda is associated with 'Tri-Pravara' and 'Panch-Pravara' as 
a prayer to invite Agni?
Rigveda.
4143
According to the Panji system, the descendants of parents, 
grandparents, and great-grandparents are collectively checked 
across _____ sources of lineage.
Thirty-two (32).
4144
Identify the geographical boundary between 'Pancha Gauda' and 
'Pancha Dravida' Brahmins.
The Vindhyachal Mountains.
4145
What is the primary difference in marriage rituals between 
'Vajasaneyi' and 'Chhandoga' Brahmins regarding the time of 
ceremony?
Chhandoga marriages must observe the stars (Arundhati) after 
sunset, while Vajasaneyi marriages can occur day or night.
4146
Which classification of Maithil Brahmins is traditionally referred to 
as 'Pachhwari-par'?
The non-Shrotriya Brahmins (Yogya and Panjibaddh).
4147
According to the Panji, what is the consequence of marrying a 
'Swajana' (a prohibited relative)?
The offspring were historically considered 'Chandala' and excluded 
from Aryan social standing.
4148
What is the 'Ghataka' in the context of marriage negotiations?
A person (often a Panjikar) who facilitates the matching and 
verification of lineages.
4149
Who is the 'Beeji' of the Mandar family lineage mentioned in the 
text?
Trayi-vidya-Bhatta.
4150
How many main 'Moolas' were selected as 'Atyanta Shrestha' (most 
elite) in the Shrotriya classification?
Thirteen (13).
4151
What was the significance of the year 1248 Saka in Mithila history? It marks the formal institution of the Panji system by Maharaja 
Harisimhadeva.
4152
Identify one of the six specific 'Chhattha' (prohibited degrees) 
related to the maternal grandmother's lineage.
The maternal grandfather of the maternal grandmother (Matamahi-
matamaha-pita).
4153
The 'Pancha Dravida' division includes Brahmins from Karnataka, 
Gujarat, Telangana, Maharashtra, and _____.
Dravida (Tamil Nadu/Kerala area).
4154
True or False: The Panji records were historically restricted to 
Brahmins only.
False (they were also maintained for Karna Kayasthas and certain 
Rajput/Kshatriya clans).
4155
The title 'Panji-Shastra-Martanda' was awarded to which notable 
genealogist by the Maithili Academy?
Modanand Jha.
4156
What was the role of the 'Siddhant Patra' in the marriage process?
It served as legal and social permission from the Panjikar to proceed 
with a wedding.
4157
In the context of Panji, what does 'Avisvajan-Patra' mean?
A document certifying that the parties are 'not of the same lineage' 
(not prohibited relatives).
4158
How many generations of the mother's side are checked for 
'Sapinda' according to the Manusmriti quoted in the text?
Five generations.
4159
Which king of the Karnata dynasty is credited with the 'Panji 
Prabandh'?
Harisimhadeva.
4160
What is 'Moola Panji'?
A record focusing on the original ancestral root and the 
geographical spread of a family.
4161
Under Maharaja Madhav Singh, which new type of Panji was 
introduced that led to further social stratification?
Shakha Panji (Branch records).
4162
The total number of Panjis for the 'Yogya' and 'Panjibaddh' 
categories is _____.
One hundred and fifty-three (153).
4163
The process of verifying sixteen specific paternal and maternal 
relations to ensure marriage eligibility is called _____.
Chhattha-Nirnaya (Deciding the six-fold relationship).

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
4164
Which Vedic sage's name is often associated with the Sandilya 
Gotra?
Sandilya.
4165
Why is the preservation of Tirhuta script considered vital to the 
Panji system?
Because the vast majority of historical Panji records are written 
exclusively in Tirhuta script.
4166
In the context of the Maithil Brahmin community, what is the 
primary purpose of the 'Panji Prabandh' system?
To ensure genealogical purity and regulate marriage eligibility.
4167
Which historical ruler is credited with officially organising the Panji 
system in Mithila in 1326 AD (Shaka 1248)?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
4168
What is the total number of primary 'Gotras' identified within the 
Maithil Brahmin community according to the source?
20
4169
According to the Panji system, how many generations must be 
avoided on the father's side for a marriage to be considered valid?
Seven (often modified to six in practice).
4170
How many generations must be avoided on the mother's side for a 
valid marriage in the Maithil Brahmin tradition?
Five.
4171
The Maithil Brahmins are classified as part of which broader group 
of the 'Pancha Gauda'?
Maithil.
4172
What is the specific role of a 'Panjikar' in Mithila?
A designated registrar or scholar who maintains genealogical 
records and issues marriage permits.
4173
Into how many 'Chhatth' (points of intersection) is the eligibility of 
a bride or groom divided to check for blood relations?
16
4174
The classification of Maithil Brahmins into Shrotriya, Yogya, and 
Panjibadh was formalised during the reign of which later ruler?
Maharaja Madhava Singh.
4175
What is the total number of 'Mulas' (roots) recorded for Maithil 
Brahmins in the Panji records?
167
4176
In Maithil Brahmin terminology, what are the followers of the 
Madhyandina branch of the Yajurveda called?
Vajasaneyi.
4177
What term is used for the followers of the Kauthuma branch of the 
Samaveda in the Maithil tradition?
Chandoga.
4178
The Panji system was originally applicable to Brahmins, Kayasthas, 
and which other group in Mithila?
Kshatriyas.
4179
Term: Bijipurnsha (Biji Purush)
Definition: The earliest known founding ancestor of a specific 
lineage or 'Mula'.
4180
How many ancestors are included in the maternal line calculation of 
the 'Panjibadh' category compared to the 'Shrotriya'?
Five generations for both (though father's side differs).
4181
What is the difference between 'Tri-pravara' and 'Pancha-pravara' in 
the context of Gotras?
Tri-pravara indicates three sage ancestors, while Pancha-pravara 
indicates five sage ancestors.
4182
The Sandilya Gotra is classified as having how many Pravaras?
Three (Tri-pravara).
4183
Vatsa and Savarna Gotras are examples of lineages following how 
many Pravaras?
Five (Pancha-pravara).
4184
What is 'Samuha-Lekhya'?
The practice of maintaining collective family records, mentioned by 
Kumarila Bhatta in the 7th century.
4185
Which historical figure was appointed by King Harisimhadeva to 
manage the Kayastha Panji?
Shankardatta.
4186
In the Maithil Brahmin hierarchy, which group is known as 
'Pachhwari-paat'?
Non-Shrotriya Brahmins (Yogya and Panjibadh).
4187
Which scholar is credited as the first chief Panjikar (registrar) of the 
Maithil Brahmins?
Gunakar Jha.
4188
What specific ecological precaution is recommended for preserving 
Panji manuscripts according to the source?
Protecting them from water, oil, and loose binding, and drying them 
in the sun when it is in the Leo zodiac sign.
4189
The first 'Chhatth' (genealogical checkpoint) for a bride represents 
the _____.
Father's paternal great-great-grandfather.
4190
Which script was traditionally used to write the Panji records in 
Mithila?
Tirhuta (Maithili script).
4191
In the context of marriage, what does the term 'Asvajana' signify?
A person who is not a sapinda or blood relative within prohibited 
degrees.
4192
Which Gotra is shared by all Karna Kayasthas in Mithila?
Kashyapa.
4193
What is 'Laukik' in the context of Shrotriya Panji?
A classification system used to determine social status based on 
marriage alliances.
4194
The 'Pancha Gauda' Brahmins are geographically separated from 
the 'Pancha Dravida' by which landmark?
Vindhyachal Mountains.
4195
Term: Mulgram
Definition: The current or most recent residential village of a 
family, distinct from their ancestral 'Mula'.
4196
What was the immediate catalyst for King Harisimhadeva to 
formalise the Panji system?
An accidental incestuous marriage involving scholar Harinath 
Upadhyaya due to lack of records.
4197
Which branch of Vedic rituals involves looking at the stars 
(Arundhati) after sunset during a marriage?
Chandoga (Samaveda).
4198
Which 'Mula' is associated with the famous Maithili poet Vidyapati? Bisapi (though the source lists lineages like 'Mandavi').

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
4199
How many 'Mahatools' or primary branches were identified among 
the 167 original Mulas?
34 (plus one, totaling 35).
4200
True or False: According to the Panji system, marriage between 
Savarna and Vatsa gotras is prohibited.
True (they share the same ancestors/pravaras).
4201
Under the Panji rules, what is the consequence of marrying within 
one's own Gotra?
The offspring is traditionally considered outcaste (Chandal status in 
historical texts).
4202
The 'Tantra-Varttika' by Kumarila Bhatta refers to records of family 
virtues and flaws as _____.
Samuha-lekhya.
4203
The 'Udegh' process requires identifying ancestors from both the 
paternal and _____ side.
Maternal.
4204
Which classification of Maithil Brahmins is traditionally considered 
the highest in social rank within the Panji system?
Shrotriya.
4205
In the list of 20 gotras, 'Kashyapa' is associated with which primary 
Pravaras?
Kashyapa, Avatsara, and Naidhruva.
4206
What is the 'Chaturvala' (four tiers) system in Panji?
The division of Shrotriyas into four distinct status groups.
4207
Which Panji record specifically tracks 'Kshina' or defects in a 
lineage over time?
Dushana Panji.
4208
Why is the year 1326 AD significant for Maithil social history?
It marks the formal establishment and recording of the 'Panji 
Prabandh'.
4209
According to the source, what is the 'Genome Mapping' period 
covered by these records?
450 AD to 2009 AD.
4210
Which gotra includes the pravaras Shakti, Vashishtha, and 
Parashara?
Parashara.
4211
The document mentions a 'Muslim' or 'Chamar' marriage in the 
lineage; where would this be recorded?
Dushana Panji.
4212
How many total ancestors are theoretically vetted in a full 'Udegh' 
check?
32
4213
In the hierarchy, 'Jaibar' refers to which group?
Those who are not officially recorded in the Panji books (non-
registered).
4214
What is the significance of the village 'Behat' in the Panji examples? It serves as a prominent 'Mula' or 'Mulgram' mentioned in ancestral 
lineages like those of Shankaridatta.
4215
The term 'Pancha Gauda' traditionally includes Brahmins from 
Saraswat, Kanyakubja, Gauda, Utkal, and _____.
Maithil.
4216
Who was the 'Biji Purush' of the Kharaure Mula?
The source identifies specific founding ancestors for each Mula, 
such as Gunakar Jha's lineage founding.
4217
True or False: A marriage is prohibited if the bride is the offspring 
of the groom's maternal uncle.
True (specifically prohibited in rule 4 of marriage eligibility).
4218
What was the purpose of creating the 'Shakha' (Branch) Panji under 
Maharaja Madhava Singh?
To differentiate between families based on their changing social 
practices and marital choices.
4219
In the process of checking 'Chhatth', if a relation is found on the 
groom's paternal side, is the marriage allowed?
No, it is prohibited.
4220
In the 'Chhatth' system, if a blood relation is found solely on the 
groom's maternal side, is marriage permitted?
Yes, provided it is beyond the prohibited five generations.
4221
Which script's preservation is considered vital to the Panji system's 
survival?
Tirhuta.
4222
What historical evidence is cited for the existence of Panji-like 
records before Harisimhadeva?
The 'Tantra-Varttika' by the 7th-century scholar Kumarila Bhatta.
4223
The source identifies 'Shrotriyas' as having how many specific 
'Panjis' (books)?
32
4224
The total number of Panjis for the 'Yogya' and 'Panjibadh' 
categories combined is _____.
153
4225
Which Maithil King's library in Nepal was searched for earlier Panji 
manuscripts?
The source references the historical migration of records toward 
Nepal libraries.
4226
Term: Paat
Definition: A social rank or tier within the Maithil Brahmin 
community, often determined by marriage history.
4227
In what year was the formal 'Panji Prabandh' system initiated in 
Mithila?
1326 AD (Saka 1248).
4228
Which ruler of the Karnat dynasty is credited with establishing the 
Panji system?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
4229
What was the primary purpose for establishing the Panji Prabandh?
To maintain genealogical purity and regulate marriage eligibility 
among Brahmins, Kayasthas, and Kshatriyas.
4230
Who was the scholar appointed by Maharaja Harisimhadeva to 
manage the Panji of Maithil Brahmins?
Gunakar Jha.
4231
Who was the designated manager for the Panji of the Kshatriyas?
Vijaydatta.
4232
How many total 'Gotras' (clans) are recognized in the Maithil 
Brahmin community?
20
4233
Define the term 'Udegh' in the context of Maithil genealogy.
An introduction of seven generations of ancestors required for 
marriage eligibility.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
4234
How many ancestors' introductions are ideally required to establish 
the 'full' lineage of a person?
32
4235
What are the three main social hierarchies established among 
Maithil Brahmins during the Panji organization?
Shrotriya, Yogya, and Panjibaddh.
4236
What term is used for those who were not formally registered in the 
Panji system?
Jaywar (or Jaibar).
4237
In the Shrotriya category, how many generations must be avoided 
on the maternal side for marriage?
Five.
4238
In the Shrotriya category, how many generations must be avoided 
on the paternal side for marriage?
Seven.
4239
How many 'Moola-Grams' (original villages) were initially 
identified in the system?
167
4240
How many 'Main Moolas' (primary roots) were determined out of 
the 167 original villages?
34 (later 35).
4241
Which branch of the Khanam moola was added as the 35th main 
moola at a later date?
Phandah.
4242
Maithil Brahmins are divided based on Vedic study into which two 
primary groups?
Chandogya (Samavedi) and Vajasaneyi (Yajurvedi).
4243
The five northern Brahmin groups are collectively known as _____. Pancha Gauda.
4244
The five southern Brahmin groups are collectively known as _____. Pancha Dravida.
4245
Which geographic boundary separates the Pancha Gauda and 
Pancha Dravida Brahmins?
The Vindhyachal mountains.
4246
Which Samavedic shakha (branch) is followed by the Chandogya 
Maithil Brahmins?
Kauthuma.
4247
Which Yajurvedic shakha (branch) is followed by the Vajasaneyi 
Maithil Brahmins?
Madhyandina.
4248
How many sacraments (Sanskars) are performed in the Upanayana 
of a Chandogya Brahmin?
Four (Namkarana, Chudakarana, Upanayana, Samavartana).
4249
Which document, first mentioned by Kumarila Bhatta, served as the 
precursor to written genealogical records?
Samuha-Lekhya.
4250
What is the 'Siddhant Patra' (or Aswajan-patra)?
A certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming that the bride and 
groom are not prohibited relatives.
4251
Who is the 'Beeji Purush' in a Panji record?
The earliest known foundational ancestor of a specific lineage or 
clan.
4252
What is 'Laukit' in the Shrotriya Panji?
Specific genealogical records used to track the sub-lineages and 
relative status within the Shrotriya category.
4253
Which Panji type specifically records the faults or 'stains' in a 
lineage?
Dushan Panji.
4254
The rule 'Matritah Panchami Tyaktwa Pitritah Saptami Bhajet' 
refers to which practice?
The prohibition of marriage within five generations of the mother's 
side and seven of the father's.
4255
How many 'Chhadwith' (specific ancestor-based connections) must 
be checked to ensure no Sapinda relation exists?
Sixteen.
4256
According to the Panji system, marriage with a girl from the 
maternal uncleâ€™s lineage is generally _____.
Prohibited (especially if within the specified generations).
4257
Which Maharaja of Darbhanga initiated the creation of 'Shakha 
Panjis' in the 18th century?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
4258
How many Gotras are commonly found among the Karna 
Kayasthas of Mithila?
One (Kashyapa).
4259
What is the 'Moola Panji'?
The foundational register recording the original village and lineage 
of every family.
4260
Which Panji version focuses on the generational distance (Sapinda) 
for marriage calculations?
Udegh Panji.
4261
What is the significance of the month of Simha-Rashi (approx. 16 
Aug to 15 Sept) for Panji preservation?
It is the time recommended to dry the palm-leaf records in the sun 
to prevent decay.
4262
The first Chhadwith relation is defined as the daughter of the bride's 
_____.
Vriddha-Prapitamaha (Great-Great-Grandfather).
4263
What is the 'Ayaachi' title associated with, in the context of scholar 
Bhavanath Mishra?
A scholar who does not accept any donations or fees for his 
services.
4264
Who is the earliest mentioned foundational ancestor (Beeji Purush) 
of the Mandar moola?
Mahamahopadhyaya Triveni Bhatta (approx. 400 AD).
4265
The 'Dushan Panji' notes social deviations, such as marriage with 
an 'Antyaja' (lower caste). What happened to the status of these 
lineages over several generations?
The fault was eventually forgotten or diluted after six generations of 
pure marriages.
4266
Which famous poet's lineage is meticulously tracked in the Panji 
records?
Vidyapati Thakur.
4267
What was the occupation of those who were specifically appointed 
to calculate and verify marriage eligibility?
Panjikars.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
4268
Which famous saint is mentioned in the Panji as a disciple of 
Damodar Upadhyaya of the Karmehe lineage?
Chaitanya Mahaprabhu.
4269
What is the 'Shrotriya' category based on according to the text?
A combination of noble lineage and the adherence to religious 
conduct (Karma-kanda).
4270
Identify the Gotra of the 'Oini' moola Brahmins.
Kashyapa.
4271
Identify the Gotra of the 'Sarisab' moola Brahmins.
Shandilya.
4272
Identify the Gotra of the 'Pali' moola Brahmins.
Vatsa.
4273
Identify the Gotra of the 'Dighe' (Dighwe) moola Brahmins.
Shandilya.
4274
The term 'Moola' in Panji indicates the ancestral _____.
Village (place of origin).
4275
The 13th Chhadwith relation refers to the bride's paternal 
grandmother's _____.
Paternal Great-Grandfather.
4276
In Panji records, if a lineage is described as 'Agnaat Maatrik' 
(unknown mother), what does it imply?
That the maternal side was not verified or was from an unregistered 
lineage.
4277
Which Panji category was specifically created to handle the records 
of the 'Soti' (Shrotriya) subgroup?
Shakha Panji.
4278
What is the total number of Yogya and Panjibaddh Panjis 
mentioned?
153
4279
How does the Panji track the transition from a 'faulty' lineage back 
to a 'pure' one?
By monitoring 'Kshayan' (decay) of the fault over subsequent pure 
generational unions.
4280
In the context of the Panji system, what does the term 'Uderh' 
represent?
The introduction and detailed pedigree of thirty-two specific 
ancestral lines required for marriage validation.
4281
How many total ancestral introductions are required to establish an 
'Uderh' for marriage eligibility?
$32$ distinct ancestral lines.
4282
According to the Panji Prabandh, what is the generation-based 
restriction for marriage on the paternal side?
Marriage is prohibited within seven generations (often interpreted 
as excluding six generations) of the father's lineage.
4283
What is the generation-based restriction for marriage on the 
maternal side in the Panji system?
Marriage is prohibited within five generations of the mother's 
lineage.
4284
Which historical ruler of Mithila is credited with the formalised 
implementation of the Panji system?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
4285
In what year ($AD$) was the decision made to formalise the 
collection of lineage records under Maharaja Harisimhadeva?
$1326$ AD ($1248$ Saka).
4286
Who was the scholar appointed by Maharaja Harisimhadeva to 
manage the Panji records for Maithil Brahmins?
Gunakar Jha of the Pandua Mula.
4287
What are the three main hierarchical categories of Maithil Brahmins 
resulting from the Panji Prabandh?
Shrotriya, Yogya, and Panjibaddh.
4288
Maithil Brahmins who were not formally recorded in the initial 
Panji system are traditionally referred to as _____.
Jaywars
4289
How many 'Gotras' are formally recognised within the Maithil 
Brahmin community according to the source material?
$20$ Gotras.
4290
How many 'Mula-gramas' (original villages) were initially identified 
in the Panji system?
$167$ Mula-gramas.
4291
In the Panji system, what does a 'Beeji Purush' signify?
The earliest known progenitor or founding ancestor of a specific 
lineage or 'Mula'.
4292
The 'Tri-pravara' classification refers to lineages that claim descent 
from how many primary sages?
$3$ primary sages.
4293
What does the term 'Pancha-pravara' indicate regarding a Gotra's 
ancestry?
Descent from $5$ primary sages.
4294
Which two Gotras are noted for having the same ancestors, thereby 
prohibiting marriage between them?
Vatsa and Savarna.
4295
What is the specific document issued by a Panjikar certifying that a 
bride and groom are not related within prohibited degrees?
Siddhanta Patra (also known as Aswajana Patra).
4296
Which philosopher's work, 'Tantra Vartika', provides an early 
reference to 'Samuha-lekhya' (collective records) used for 
preserving caste purity?
Kumarila Bhatta.
4297
What are the five regions traditionally comprising the 'Pancha 
Gauda' Brahmin group north of the Vindhyas?
Utkala, Kanyakubja, Gauda, Maithil, and Saraswat.
4298
Which Veda and branch (shakha) do 'Vajasaneyi' Maithil Brahmins 
follow?
The Madhyandina shakha of the Shukla Yajurveda.
4299
According to the Panji system's marriage rules, a groom must not be 
the offspring of his maternal uncle's _____.
Brother (maternal uncle's children).
4300
The Panji system classifies 'Gotras' into how many distinct 
categories based on their 'Pravara' count?
Two ($3$ and $5$).
4301
Which Gotra includes the Mulas 'Dighwa', 'Sarisab', and 'Mahua'?
Sandilya.
4302
What is the 'Mula' of a Maithil Brahmin primarily based upon?
The ancestral village or place of origin.
4303
What is the function of the 'Dushana Panji'?
To record flaws or 'kshati' (lineage blemishes) that occurred within 
specific families.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
4304
The 'Sakha Panji' system, which introduced more detailed sub-
branch records, began during the reign of which King?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
4305
In the Panji records, what information does the 'Uderh Panji' 
provide regarding Sapinda relations?
It traces back six generations to identify common ancestors.
4306
Which scholar's lineage rules, stating the $7^{th}$ generation on 
the father's side is eligible for marriage, became the accepted social 
standard?
Mahamahopadhyaya Mahesh Thakur.
4307
What does 'Chhadwith' refer to in the context of marriage eligibility 
verification?
Sixteen specific ancestral relationships checked by the Panjikar to 
ensure no prohibited connection exists.
4308
Why were the Brahmin communities geographically divided into 
'Pancha Gauda' and 'Pancha Dravida'?
Due to regional influences like climate, language, and dietary habits 
over time.
4309
Which specific ancestor represents the 'First Chhadwith' for a girl's 
marriage eligibility check?
The great-grandfather of her great-great-grandfather ($Vriddha 
Prapitamaha$).
4310
What defines the 'Beeji Purush' of the Sandilya Gotra lineage from 
the village 'Gangoli'?
Gangadhar ($1/1$).
4311
Among Karna Kayasthas, which Gotra is universally followed 
according to the Panji records?
Kashyap.
4312
What was the role of 'Vijayadatta' in the Panji system's inception?
He was appointed to manage the Panji records for the Kshatriya 
community.
4313
What was the role of 'Shankardatta' in the Panji system's inception?
He was appointed to manage the Panji records for the Karna 
Kayastha community.
4314
How does the marriage ritual timing differ between 'Vajasaneyi' 
and 'Chhandogya' Maithil Brahmins?
Chhandogyas perform the 'Uttara Vivaha' only after sunset after 
seeing the Arundhati star, while Vajasaneyis have no such time 
restriction.
4315
In the Panji system, what is the term for a marriage that occurs 
within the same 'Mula' and 'Gotra'?
Swajana (prohibited/incestuous in this social context).
4316
What is the penalty mentioned in ancient smritis for children born 
of 'Swajana' (prohibited) marriages?
They were historically categorised as 'Chandala' and excluded from 
Aryan social rites.
4317
Which document is considered the life-force of the 'Tirhuta' script 
according to the source material?
The Panji Prabandh records.
4318
The scholars who maintain and verify the Panji records are 
professionally known as _____.
Panjikars
4319
Identify the Gotra belonging to the 'Beeji Purush' M.M. Halayudha.
Simhashram.
4320
Which Maithil Brahmin Gotra lists 'Parashar' as one of its five 
primary sages (Pancha-pravara)?
Parashar (Tri-pravara) or specific branches within Vatsa/Savarna.
4321
According to the Panji records, which famous Bhakti saint was the 
disciple of Damodar Upadhyaya of the Karmehe lineage?
Chaitanya Mahaprabhu.
4322
What is the significance of the year $1644$ Saka ($1722$ AD) in 
the 'Uttari-Teeph' records?
It marks the date of compilation for the specific branch records 
mentioned by Devandand Jha.
4323
How many primary 'Mulas' were selected as 'Main Mulas' out of the 
$167$ recorded?
$34$ (later $35$ including the Khanam branch of Phandah).
4324
The 'Laukik' refers to the specific lineage records maintained for 
which category of Maithil Brahmins?
Shrotriya Brahmins.
4325
In the Panji hierarchy, the 'Pachhwari Paith' refers to which group?
The non-Shrotriya Brahmins (Yogya and Panjibaddh).
4326
What is the meaning of 'Arsha' in the context of Pravaras?
Relating to or derived from the Rishis (sages).
4327
Which Gotra is associated with the Beeji Purush 'Trivikram Bhatta'? Mandir.
4328
In the verification process, if a 'Chhadwith' relationship is found in 
the groom's paternal side, is the marriage permitted?
No, it is prohibited.
4329
If a common ancestor (Chhadwith) is found in the groom's maternal 
side beyond the prohibited generations, is the marriage permitted?
Yes, it is generally permitted.
4330
Name the four essential sacraments ($Samskaras$) performed for 
Samavedi (Chhandogya) Brahmin initiation.
Namakarana, Chudakarana, Upanayana, and Samavartana.
4331
How does the initiation ($Upanayana$) of a Vajasaneyi Brahmin 
differ from a Chhandogya?
It includes 'Vedarmbha' as a distinct fourth sacrament in the 
sequence.
4332
What does the 'Gotra Panji' primarily contain?
A comprehensive list of all Gotras and their associated ancient 
Mulas.
4333
The 'Dushana' recorded in lineages can include historical instances 
of marriages with _____.
Unknown castes or prohibited relationships (e.g., 'Ajnat Kula').
4334
What was the purpose of the 'Paithi' system introduced by Maharaja 
Madhav Singh?
To establish social gradation and determine hierarchical status 
within the community.
4335
The 'Beeji' of the 'Khauwal' Mula is associated with which village?
Oini.
4336
Which scholar is noted as the '$18^{th}$ generation' descendant of 
the Panji system's founder, Gunakar Jha?
Modanand Jha.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
4337
The physical Panji records were traditionally written on which 
material?
Palm leaves (Taal-patra) or Basaha-patra.
4338
Which ink/substance was traditionally cautioned against in the 
preservation sloka for Panji books?
Oil (Tailat Raksha).
4339
According to the Panji preservation rules, during which astrological 
period should the books be dried in the sun?
When the Sun is in the Leo (Simha) rashi (approx. mid-August to 
mid-September).
4340
The 'Pancha Dravida' Brahmins include groups from which regions? Karnat, Gurjar, Tailang, Dravid, and Maharashtra.
4341
Which sage is the progenitor of the 'Kashyap' Gotra?
Kashyap.
4342
Lineages claiming the 'Pancha-pravara' sages Aurva, Chyavana, 
Bhargava, Jamadagnya, and Apnuvana belong to which Gotras?
Vatsa or Savarna.
4343
What was the historical title of the scholars who issued the 
'Aswajana Patra'?
Panjikar.
4344
Which philosopher mentioned that 'Maha-kulinas' (those of high 
birth) preserve their caste through 'Samuha-lekhya'?
Kumarila Bhatta.
4345
The 'Khanum' branch of the Phandah Mula is associated with which 
social rank in later periods?
Higher lineage/Uchcha Vamsha.
4346
The Panji system originally included records for how many Rajput 
(Kshatriya) clans?
$72$ (with $64$ described in 'Varna Ratnakara').
4347
Which Maithil poetâ€™s lineage is tracked for over $19$ 
generations in the Panji records?
Vidyapati Thakur.
4348
In the context of the Panji, what is the 'Mul-gram'?
The primary village associated with the founding of a lineage 
branch.
4349
Who was the 'Beeji Purush' of the 'Budhwal' Mula?
Vasudeva.
4350
In the Panji hierarchy, which category is considered the highest?
Shrotriya.
4351
What was the primary purpose of the 'Panji Prabandh' system 
introduced in medieval Mithila?
To ensure caste purity and determine eligibility for marriage among 
Brahmins, Kayasthas, and Kshatriyas.
4352
According to the source, the 'Uteh' involved the introduction of how 
many generations of ancestors?
Seven generations.
4353
To determine marriage eligibility, a person's lineage (Uteh) required 
knowledge of how many 'Kuls' or ancestral lineages?
32 Kuls.
4354
In the Mithila social hierarchy, what were the three main divisions 
of Brahmins resulting from the Panji system?
Shrotriya, Yogya, and Panjibadh.
4355
What term was used to describe those who were not formally 
registered in the Panji system?
Jaywar.
4356
For marriage eligibility, how many generations must typically be 
avoided on the mother's side?
Five generations.
4357
The Panji system records a total of how many original 'Gots' 
(Gotras) among Maithil Brahmins?
20 Gotras.
4358
The Panji system records how many original 'Muls' (village 
lineages) among Maithil Brahmins?
167 Muls.
4359
Out of the 167 'Muls', how many were designated as 'Mukhya' 
(primary) Muls?
34 Muls.
4360
Define 'Pravara' in the context of the Panji system.
The specific number of ancestors (usually three or five) who were 
original composers of Rigvedic hymns.
4361
Which three Gotras are classified as 'Panchapravara' (having five 
Pravaras)?
Vatsa, Savarna, and Garga.
4362
Name the three ancestors associated with the Shandilya Gotra.
Shandilya, Asita, and Devala.
4363
Why is marriage traditionally prohibited between members of the 
Vatsa and Savarna Gotras?
Because they share the same common ancestors.
4364
In the 10-fold classification of Indian Brahmins, Maithil Brahmins 
belong to which broader group?
Pancha Gauda.
4365
What was the administrative name of the region today known as 
Mithila in ancient times?
Videha.
4366
Which philosopher's work 'Tantravartika' first mentioned 'Samuh-
lekhya' (collective written lineage records)?
Kumarila Bhatta.
4367
The current organised form of the Panji system was established 
under the reign of which King?
King Harisimhadeva.
4368
In what year (Shaka and Gregorian) was the Panji Prabandh 
officially established by Royal decree?
Shaka 1248, which is 1326 AD.
4369
What event prompted the creation of the Panji system in the 14th 
century?
Pandit Harinath Upadhyaya unknowingly married a close relative 
due to the lack of written records.
4370
What is the title of the official appointed by the King to verify 
marriage eligibility using Panji records?
Panjikar.
4371
Define 'Bijipurush' in the Mithila Panji system.
The earliest known ancestor from whom a specific lineage or 'Mul' 
originates.
4372
What does the 'Mulgram' signify in a person's introduction?
The original village associated with the family's lineage.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
4373
Among Maithil Brahmins, which Gotra has the highest number of
